work:
# Weekly Blockchain and Fintech News Recap

## UBS Advances Blockchain Technology for Gold Trading

UBS, a global leader in financial services, has entered the trial phase of integrating blockchain technology into its gold trading operations. By exploring the use of blockchain, UBS aims to enhance transparency, security, and efficiency in the gold market. Managing secure transactions while reducing conventional manual verifications could revolutionize the precious metals sector.

## 21Shares Applies for a Polkadot ETF with the SEC

21Shares, a prominent cryptocurrency ETP issuer, has submitted an application to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) for a Polkadot Exchange-Traded Fund (ETF). This move aligns with the growing interest in Polkadot’s interoperability and scalability features, promising to provide investors with regulated access to DOT, Polkadot’s native cryptocurrency.

## Purpose Investments Proposes World’s First XRP-ETF in Canada

In a groundbreaking initiative, Purpose Investments seeks to launch the world’s first XRP-ETF in Canada. This application to Canadian regulatory authorities reflects the increasing mainstream acceptance of Ripple’s XRP as more investors look to diversify their cryptocurrency portfolios with regulated investment products.

## Jupiter Mobile Unveils the Magic Scan Feature

Jupiter Mobile has introduced an innovative new feature called Magic Scan, enhancing user experience via advanced scanning capabilities. This feature allows users to quickly and securely interact with documents and images, potentially integrating further with decentralized technologies to bolster security and efficiency in digital transactions.

## World Liberty Financial Boosts Ethereum Investments

World Liberty Financial, a forward-thinking financial institution, has doubled its investments in Ethereum. This strategic move supports Ethereum's continued growth as a leading platform for decentralized applications (dApps), smart contracts, and its anticipated transition to Ethereum 2.0, enhancing scalability and energy efficiency.

## Tether Achieves Remarkable Profitability

Tether, a major stablecoin provider, reported an impressive profit of $85.6 million per employee. This high profitability underscores the efficiency of Tether's operations amid rising adoption of stablecoins for trading, remittances, and as a hedge against cryptocurrency market volatility.

## Shiba Inu Saw a 7240% Burn Rate Increase

The Shiba Inu community achieved a significant milestone with a reported 7240% rise in their token burn rate. This strategy is aimed at reducing the circulating supply of SHIB tokens, potentially increasing scarcity and value for holders in the long term.

## Web3 and Blockchain Projects Attract Over $1 Billion in January

The start of the year has been robust for Web3 and blockchain projects, amassing over $1 billion in fresh investments. This influx of capital reflects growing confidence and innovation in decentralized technologies, which continue to attract interest from venture capitalists and traditional sectors alike.

## Sky Protocol's Accelerating Growth

Amid an increasing supply of USDS, Sky Protocol is experiencing rapid growth. Sky Protocol offers a decentralized data economy solution, enhancing security and data privacy. Its expansion is buoyed by the demand for transparent and reliable financial data services.

## AI Bounty Program by TON and ElizaOS

The Open Network (TON) in collaboration with ElizaOS, announces an AI Bounty Program to support the development of AI technologies within the blockchain framework. This initiative seeks to encourage innovative AI solutions, fostering growth and integration into the TON ecosystem.

---

This detailed recap provides a comprehensive overview of the latest movements in blockchain and related technologies, demonstrating the sector's dynamic and evolving landscape. Please review the individual sections for specific insights into the actions and implications for each organization involved.
